title: Structs - bundling fields into one type
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: A struct groups related values into a single type. You reach a field with a dot.
explanation: When values belong together - an x and a y, a name and an age - a struct names that bundle as one type. You define the shape once, then make variables of it and reach inside with a dot: p.x. (When you have a pointer to a struct, you use -> instead, which is just a tidy way of writing (*ptr).field.) Structs are how C builds bigger ideas out of the small types.

exercises.question: What does this print?
hints: p was made with {3, 4} - x then y.

exercises.question: How do you reach a field of a struct variable?
Through a pointer to a struct, which operator reaches a field?
